channel.title: NPR Topics: Health
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trying to avoid cyclosporiasis? Wash your hands, wash your produce
item.description: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly talks with Kali Kniel, professor of microbial food safety at the University of Delaware, about recent outbreaks of cyclosporiasis across the U.S.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly talks with Kali Kniel, professor of microbial food safety at the University of Delaware, about recent outbreaks of cyclosporiasis across the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Jason Fuller

item.title: There's no treatment designed for the Ebola strain ravaging DRC. But now there's hope
item.description: The strain of the virus that's responsible for the current outbreak has no specific treatments or preventive measures. Three new clinical trials could provide possible breakthroughs.
item.content:encoded:
The strain of the virus that's responsible for the current outbreak has no specific treatments or preventive measures. Three new clinical trials could provide possible breakthroughs.

(Image credit: Daniel Buuma)
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Lambert

item.title: Trump administration cancels grants aimed at reducing teen pregnancies
item.description: The Trump administration has canceled all but a handful of grants meant to teach teenagers and caregivers about reducing teen pregnancies, arguing they are "normalizing sexual activity for minors."
item.content:encoded: The Trump administration has canceled all but a handful of grants meant to teach teenagers and caregivers about reducing teen pregnancies, arguing they are "normalizing sexual activity for minors."
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin

item.title: What a divorce coach wishes couples knew before ending a marriage
item.description: Divorce is a tool, not a weapon, says Karen McNenny, author of a new book on the subject. She explains how to end a marriage while protecting your family and your mental health.
item.content:encoded:
Divorce is a tool, not a weapon, says Karen McNenny, author of a new book on the subject. She explains how to end a marriage while protecting your family and your mental health.

(Image credit: Wiley/Jossey-Bass/NPR, Nicole Wickens/NPR)
item.dc:creator: Marielle Segarra

item.title: Get your reading glasses... in the post office
item.description: In some towns in India, people working for a non profit approach people who appear to have trouble reading small print to get them hooked up with glasses.
item.content:encoded: In some towns in India, people working for a non profit approach people who appear to have trouble reading small print to get them hooked up with glasses.
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel

item.title: Kenya grapples with reduced U.S. aid
item.description: Purity Wamboi was diagnosed with TB when it was pretty far advanced. It's possible that had health funding from the U.S. not been reduced, the illness may have been caught in time to save her.
item.content:encoded: Purity Wamboi was diagnosed with TB when it was pretty far advanced. It's possible that had health funding from the U.S. not been reduced, the illness may have been caught in time to save her.
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: These Medicare beneficiaries thought their drug plan was free. Then they lost it
item.description: Thousands of people lost coverage over as little as $8 in delinquent payments. They didn't know their zero-dollar premiums had gone up and they owed money. Most now can't get coverage until 2027.
item.content:encoded:
Thousands of people lost coverage over as little as $8 in delinquent payments. They didn't know their zero-dollar premiums had gone up and they owed money. Most now can't get coverage until 2027.  

(Image credit: fstop123/iStockphoto)
item.dc:creator: Susan Jaffe

item.title: Fast walkers in their 80s cut their risk of cognitive decline by half, a study finds
item.description: A new study of people 80 and older with exceptional gaits finds fast walkers have about a 50% lower risk of cognitive decline, showing the connection between physical health and brain health.
item.content:encoded:
A new study of people 80 and older with exceptional gaits finds fast walkers have about a 50% lower risk of cognitive decline, showing the connection between physical health and brain health.

(Image credit: FG Trade)
item.dc:creator: Allison Aubrey

item.title: Restrictions could be lifted on peptides loved by wellness influencers
item.description: Unproven peptides touting wellness have been relegated to a gray market because of Biden-era restrictions. Under the Trump administration, that could change.
item.content:encoded: Unproven peptides touting wellness have been relegated to a gray market because of Biden-era restrictions. Under the Trump administration, that could change.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone

item.title: Construction is a man's game. These women are demolishing the barriers
item.description: Women make up a miniscule 3% of construction workers in Kenya. But a nonprofit group is offering them a way to get into the game ... and to get ahead.
item.content:encoded:
Women make up a miniscule 3% of construction workers in Kenya. But a nonprofit group is offering them a way to get into the game ... and to get ahead.

(Image credit: Tommy Trenchard for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Christopher Clark
